surface_verification.justification: Framework-internal SessionStart hook (check_drift.py); no startable web/cli/api app surface. The removed timestamp detector and retained content-drift + triage resolve path are driven end-to-end by the subprocess integration test test_check_drift_hook.py::TestCheckDriftHook::test_integration_hook_composes_content_drift_and_retires_timestamp (category:integration). Full suites green: shared/tests 3596, shared/scripts/tests 196, shared/scripts/tools/tests 61; pinned-ruff clean; anti-ratchet baselines ratcheted down (check_drift.py 534->432, test_hooks.py 551->482, test_drift_triage_emit.py 415->405, test_wp5_hook_resolvers.py 304->281).

degraded.reason: medium iterate normally runs external LLM plan review; external-review keys not configured in this interactive session, so it fell back to the internal shipwright-build:code-reviewer (verdict APPROVE, one minor baseline-hygiene finding fixed) per degraded-mode.

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: timestamp symbols (check_timestamp_drift, get_newest_in_dir, get_mtime, KEY_FILES, KEY_DIRS) + the main() timestamp warning block are removed from check_drift.py
behaviors.evidence: repo-wide grep -> 0 .py references; py_compile OK; ruff@0.15.15 clean; behaviour pinned by shared/tests/test_check_drift_hook.py::test_producer_never_emits_timestamp_item PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-2: _emit_drift_to_triage drops the timestamp_drifted parameter and never emits a new :timestamp triage item
AC-3: the resolve pass dismisses pre-existing open legacy :timestamp items and leaves artifact_sync's :artifact items untouched
AC-4: content-drift detection (structure + command) is unchanged: same warnings, same :content triage items, hook exits 0
AC-5: no Timestamp-drift warning even when a former KEY_FILE (pyproject.toml) is modified more recently than CLAUDE.md
AC-6: docs/hooks-and-pipeline.md no longer describes a timestamp detector
behaviors.reason: doc prose correctness; verified by grep (0 'Timestamp drift' rows remain in docs/) + manual read. guide.md was already content-only, no change needed.

behaviors.behavior: Integration composition: the SessionStart hook subprocess composes content-drift detection + triage :content emission + resolve-pass retirement of a pre-seeded legacy :timestamp item, exit 0
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_check_drift_hook.py::TestCheckDriftHook::test_integration_hook_composes_content_drift_and_retires_timestamp PASSED (real subprocess end-to-end against a fixture Shipwright project)
